# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'BUILD_COMMAND', defaultValue: 'echo "No custom build command provided. Default action: Do nothing."', description: 'Custom shell command(s) for the build step. Example: "mvn clean install -DskipTests"')
        string(name: 'TEST_SCRIPT', defaultValue: 'echo "No custom test script provided. Default action: Do nothing."', description: 'Custom shell command(s) for the test step. Example: "mvn test"')
        string(name: 'DEPLOYMENT_STEPS', defaultValue: 'echo "No custom deployment steps provided. Default action: Do nothing."', description: 'Custom shell command(s) for the deployment step. Example: "ssh user@host \'deploy_app.sh Production\'"')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                echo "Executing custom build command from parameter: BUILD_COMMAND"
                sh "${params.BUILD_COMMAND}"
            }
        }

        stage('Test') {
            steps {
                echo "Executing custom test script from parameter: TEST_SCRIPT"
                sh "${params.TEST_SCRIPT}"
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy') {
            steps {
                echo "Executing custom deployment steps from parameter: DEPLOYMENT_STEPS"
                sh "${params.DEPLOYMENT_STEPS}"
            }
        }
    }
}
